steps.action: Create API key with health:read scope via session cookie
POST /api/shares with read-only API key (Bearer header)
steps.expected: 403 with INSUFFICIENT_SCOPES error mentioning missing shares:write scope
steps.observed: 500 INTERNAL_ERROR - endpoint uses getRequestContext (not getResolvedContext) so API key auth is not resolved, causing __api_key_pending__ userId to hit DB and crash

VAL-KEY-006.evidence: curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/shares -H 'Authorization: Bearer tot_live_xxx_yyy' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"label":"test","allowed_metrics":["sleep_score"],"data_start":"2026-01-01","data_end":"2026-01-31","expires_in_days":7}' -> 500 {"error":{"code":"INTERNAL_ERROR","message":"An unexpected error occurred"}}
issues: POST /api/shares uses getRequestContext() instead of getResolvedContext(), so API key Bearer tokens are not resolved. The middleware sets userId='__api_key_pending__' which causes a 500 error instead of properly authenticating and then enforcing scopes.
Additionally, POST /api/shares does not call enforceScope(ctx, 'shares:write'), so even if API key auth were resolved, scope enforcement would not occur.

steps.action: Create API key with health:read scope via session cookie
Use key to access GET /api/keys (key is valid but wrong scope)
steps.expected: Key authenticates (403 for scope, not 401 for auth)
steps.observed: 403 INSUFFICIENT_SCOPES - confirms key IS authenticated, just wrong scope

steps.action: Use expired key to access GET /api/keys

VAL-KEY-007.evidence: Before expiry: curl GET /api/keys -H 'Authorization: Bearer key' -> 403 INSUFFICIENT_SCOPES (authenticated but wrong scope). After docker exec totus-db psql -U totus -d totus -c "UPDATE api_keys SET expires_at = '2020-01-01' WHERE id = 'key-id'": curl GET /api/keys -H 'Authorization: Bearer key' -> 401 UNAUTHORIZED

steps.action: Create parent API key with scopes [health:read, keys:write] via session cookie
Use parent key to create child key with [shares:write] scope (not in parent scopes)
steps.expected: 403 INSUFFICIENT_SCOPES with message about broader scopes
steps.observed: 403 {"error":{"code":"INSUFFICIENT_SCOPES","message":"Cannot create an API key with broader scopes than the creating key. Requested: [shares:write], Available: [health:read, keys:write]"}}

steps.action: Use parent key to create child key with [health:read] scope (subset of parent)

VAL-KEY-010.evidence: curl -X POST /api/keys -H 'Authorization: Bearer parent_key' -d '{"name":"escalated","scopes":["shares:write"]}' -> 403 INSUFFICIENT_SCOPES. curl -X POST /api/keys -H 'Authorization: Bearer parent_key' -d '{"name":"valid","scopes":["health:read"]}' -> 201

steps.action: Verify 0 existing active keys, then create 10 keys via session cookie
steps.observed: All 10 keys created successfully (3 from earlier tests + 7 new = 10 total), all returned 201
400 {"error":{"code":"KEY_LIMIT_REACHED","message":"Maximum 10 active API keys allowed. Revoke existing keys to create new ones."}}

VAL-KEY-011.evidence: Created 10 active keys (all 201). curl -X POST /api/keys -d '{"name":"limit-test-11","scopes":["health:read"]}' -> 400 KEY_LIMIT_REACHED

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 3 passed (VAL-KEY-007, VAL-KEY-010, VAL-KEY-011), 1 failed (VAL-KEY-006: POST /api/shares returns 500 instead of 403 because it doesn't support API key auth resolution or scope enforcement).
